title: gc series-status: single command for checking series CI and review status
description: For the 'check series status' workflow, you currently need gc series-comments (to find patches), gc info (per patch, for CI/review), gc extract (for unresolved comments), and gc maloo (for CI details). A single gc series-status <url> command should walk the parent chain back to master and return for each patch: subject, patchset, CI pass/fail summary, review scores, unresolved comment count, and parent commit.

title: BUG #7: EC recovery parity matrix mismatch

title: Harnesses and agents

title: Improve project planning
description: Better project planning tooling and workflows. Includes defining how beads, epics, and dependencies are structured for multi-agent work.

title: CLI Gerrit watcher
description: A CLI agent that periodically watches a Gerrit change, examines test failures, and reports status. Needs planning: should integrate with gc tool, Maloo results, and potentially Claude Code CLI for automated analysis.

title: Maloo CLI access
description: CLI tool for querying Maloo test results directly, rather than copy-pasting from the web UI. Test results should be programmatically accessible for agents and the Gerrit watcher.
notes:
Related to jira-1o3.2 (CLI Gerrit watcher) - Maloo data feeds into the watcher but doesn't block it.
MVP implemented: session, failures, subtests, review commands. Credentials at ~/.config/maloo-tool/.env.

title: Add bug_links command (view and create)
description: Add commands to view and create bug links on test sets and subtests via the Maloo API POST /api/bug_links endpoint.

title: Add log download command
description: Add command to download test logs via the Maloo API download=true parameter.

title: Add related sessions lookup
description: Add command to find related sessions via cumulative_result_id.

title: Add retest command
description: Request retests via the Maloo web form (CSRF + POST to /test_sessions/<id>/retest). Implemented: single, all, livedebug options.

title: Remember last-used URL in session
description: Store last-used URL so subsequent commands don't need to repeat it. gc comments URL; gc reply 0 done (uses remembered URL).
close_reason: Added LastURLManager; gc comments saves URL, gc reply uses it automatically

title: Add gc examples command
description: Show common usage patterns and examples. Help LLMs understand typical workflows.
close_reason: Added gc examples command with workflows: quick, staging, series, reviewers

title: Add watch/unwatch support

title: P0: patch_watcher — add unit tests for orchestrator.py
description: orchestrator.py has 514 lines of production code with zero tests. Add unit tests for: prompt generation, decision parsing (JSONL + markdown fence stripping), config loading, error recovery paths. Target: bring code:test ratio below 1.5.

title: jira: add subtask support
description: Add 'jira create-subtask KEY --summary TEXT' and 'jira subtasks KEY' to create and list subtasks. Uses POST /rest/api/2/issue with parent field.

title: gc info: include unresolved comment count and summaries
description: gc info doesn't show unresolved comments — you have to call gc extract separately just to find out there's 1 unresolved comment. Add unresolved_count and a brief summary (file, line, author, first ~100 chars) to the info output. This covers 90% of the 'check review status' use case in one call.

title: gc series-comments walks wrong direction — includes children instead of walking back to master
description: series-comments finds the tip (furthest child) then walks backward from there, so it includes patches ABOVE the target from other developers. The series should walk BACKWARD from the target to the first MERGED commit. Backward is always a line (one parent), forward is a tree (multiple children). Current behavior returned 151 related changes including abandoned forks when user just wanted the 7 dependency patches below their change.
close_reason: Walk backward from target, not tip. Excludes unrelated children above.

description:

- Stress/repeatability loop (50-100 iterations, catch intermittent races)
- Stripe rotation collision in PFL (same OST at different stripe indices in different components)
- lfs mirror verify in degraded mode (admin tooling correctness)
- Directory-inherited EC layout recovery (layout inheritance end-to-end)
- Stripe rotation across files (multiple inodes, different starting stripes, same OST down)

Each item should eventually become its own bead.

title: test_47a: multi-component PFL EC recovery produces wrong data
description:
test_47a ("Multiple EC components full recovery") fails with md5sum mismatch.

## Test Description
test_47a creates a PFL file with two EC components:
  -E 16M -S 1M -c 2 --ec 2+1 -E -1 -S 1M -c 4 --ec 4+2
Writes 32MB, resyncs parity, deactivates one data OST per component, reads back and checks md5sum.

## Current Status
- Tests 42a-46d all PASS (single-component EC recovery works perfectly)
- test_47a FAILS: md5sum mismatch after recovery of the second PFL component (4+2)
- First component (2+1) recovers correctly
- Single-component 4+2 EC files recover correctly
- The bug is specific to the SECOND PFL component in a multi-component layout

## Root Cause Investigation (in progress)

### What We Know
1. Only the recovered stripe data is wrong (available stripes read correctly)
2. Corruption starts at exactly 1M into comp2 (start of the deactivated stripe)
3. All 4 recovery groups in comp2 produce wrong recovered data
4. Data is not zeros, not XOR, not from another stripe - it is garbage from wrong EC decode

### Instrumented Debug Output (key findings)
From CWARN instrumentation (saved in save.uncommitted_with_debug.diff):
- ec_outer_pos = 17M (NOT 0 or 16M) — VFS reads in 1M chunks
- ptrs[0..2] contain correct data stripe values
- ptrs[3] = 0000000000000000 — PARITY DATA IS ALL ZEROS
- parity_used[0] = 0 (parity "successfully" read but contains zeros)
- parity read did not error (no "fail to read parity pages" message)

### Leading Theory: Parity sub-IO uses wrong offset range
In lov_ecio_add_parity_sub(), the parity sub-IO range calculation:
```c
sub_start = cnt * lsme->lsme_stripe_size + lle->lle_extent->e_start;
```
For comp2 parity (e_start=16M): sub_start = 0 + 16M = 16M (FILE offset).
But data sub-IOs from lov_stripe_intersects get SUB-OBJECT offsets.

The parity sub-IO gets crw_pos=16M via lov_io_sub_inherit. When the parity
page at page_idx=0 is submitted through cl_io_submit_rw, the page reads
from sub-object offset 0 (correct based on page_idx). However, the sub-IO's
crw_pos=16M may cause the read to go to offset 16M in the parity sub-object,
which is beyond the object size (only 4M of parity data), returning zeros.

For comp1 parity (e_start=0), sub_start=0 which is correct by coincidence.
This explains why comp1 recovery works but comp2 does not.

### Proposed Fix
Remove `+ lle->lle_extent->e_start` from both sub_start and sub_end
calculations in lov_ecio_add_parity_sub(). This makes them sub-object
relative offsets matching what lov_io_sub_inherit expects.

Specifically, change lines ~1491-1492 and ~1525-1527 in lustre/lov/lov_io.c:
```c
// BEFORE:
sub_start = cnt * lsme->lsme_stripe_size + lle->lle_extent->e_start;
sub_end = cnt * lsme->lsme_stripe_size + lle->lle_extent->e_start;

// AFTER:
sub_start = cnt * lsme->lsme_stripe_size;
sub_end = cnt * lsme->lsme_stripe_size;
```

NOTE: This theory needs verification. The actual fix may involve the
cl_io_submit_rw path rather than (or in addition to) the sub-IO range.
Trace the full submit path: cl_io_submit_rw -> osc_io_submit -> brw RPC
to confirm whether crw_pos or page_idx determines the read offset.

## Key Files
- lustre/lov/lov_io.c — all EC recovery code
- lustre/tests/sanity-ec.sh — test_47a at lines 4590-4662
- save.uncommitted_with_debug.diff — debug instrumentation for re-use

## Test Environment
- rocky802 with OSTCOUNT=8
- Repo: /shared/master_checkouts/2
- Load: sudo -E OSTCOUNT=8 bash lustre/tests/llmount.sh
- Run: sudo -E ONLY=47a bash lustre/tests/sanity-ec.sh

## Full Commit History (12 commits)
1. 5d17b34161 LU-12669 ec: recover data from parity (original feature)
2. ab8967eaa8 LU-12669 tests: add EC recovery tests
3. 8e69c95abc LU-12669 tests: Add EC recovery tests for varied failures
4. 0d2ff0b07d LU-12669 tests: add EC recovery tests for layouts
5. 4ac03d1515 LU-12669 tests: add EC recovery stress tests
6. c48a805643 LU-12669 lov: fix OOB access in EC recovery for PFL layouts
7. 8bff08fd78 LU-12669 lov: continue lock enqueue on EC read stripe failure
8. 5eac35620c LU-12669 llite: handle EAGAIN from glimpse during EC reads
9. 57fbed3712 LU-12669 llite: always deliver data in vvp_io_ec_rd_end
10. 94dc1a182e LU-12669 lov: zero-fill pages beyond EOF in EC recovery
11. f8ce8ec4fa LU-0000 llite: fix infinite loop in ll_readpage for EC reads
12. adeeabef3e LU-0000 llite: constrain EC recovery read to component range
13. c2de62d955 LU-0000 llite: check lock coverage in ll_readpage for EC reads
14. b9a1cbc37f LU-0000 lov: fix EC recovery skipping pages for aligned reads
15. 031bd2122d LU-0000 lov: fix parity offset calculation in EC recovery
16. de9d7e2a30 LU-0000 utils: zero stripe buffers in EC parity
17. d9492d380f LU-0000 lov: handle unavailable parity in EC recovery

## Known Side Issue
osc_lru_del assertion failure during `rm` after EC recovery — LRU
accounting bug, separate from the recovery correctness issue.
notes:
## Updated Commit Hashes (after LU-12669 rewrite)
Commits 11-17 now have new hashes due to filter-branch:
11. 82c6a0c894 LU-12669 llite: fix infinite loop in ll_readpage
12. b800b368ff LU-12669 llite: constrain EC recovery read
13. df0350e7b1 LU-12669 llite: check lock coverage in ll_readpage
14. 155041699d LU-12669 lov: fix EC recovery skipping pages
15. 0020d0d0fc LU-12669 lov: fix parity offset calculation
16. d86f907382 LU-12669 utils: zero stripe buffers in EC parity
17. 0bd0c615bf LU-12669 lov: handle unavailable parity

## Full Reproduce/Test Instructions (cold start)
```bash
# 1. Build and install
ssh rocky802 'cd /shared/master_checkouts/2; sudo make -j 16 install'

# 2. Load Lustre with 8 OSTs
ssh rocky802 'cd /shared/master_checkouts/2; sudo bash lustre/tests/llmountcleanup.sh; sudo lustre_rmmod; sudo -E OSTCOUNT=8 bash lustre/tests/llmount.sh'

# 3. Run just test_47a
ssh rocky802 'cd /shared/master_checkouts/2; sudo -E ONLY=47a bash lustre/tests/sanity-ec.sh'

# 4. Run full EC test suite for regression
ssh rocky802 'cd /shared/master_checkouts/2; sudo -E ONLY=42a,42b,42c,42d,43a,43b,43c,43d,44a,44b,44c,44d,45a,45b,45c,45d,45e,46a,46b,46c,46d,47a bash lustre/tests/sanity-ec.sh'
```
Expected: 42a-46d PASS, 47a FAIL.

title: EC recovery LOV data corruption fixes (5 commits)
description:
Fixed 4 bugs in the LOV layer that caused data corruption during EC recovery. All in /shared/master_checkouts/2.

## Commits (in application order)

- c48a805643 LU-12669 lov: fix OOB access in EC recovery for PFL layouts (foundation)
- 94dc1a182e LU-12669 lov: zero-fill pages beyond EOF in EC recovery (foundation)
- b9a1cbc37f LU-0000 lov: fix EC recovery skipping pages for aligned reads
- 031bd2122d LU-0000 lov: fix parity offset calculation in EC recovery
- d9492d380f LU-0000 lov: handle unavailable parity in EC recovery

## Bug Details

### BUG 1 (b9a1cbc37f): pg_seq_end modulo wraps to zero
- File: lustre/lov/lov_io.c ~line 2327
- When read size is exact multiple of stripe_size, (crw_bytes >> PAGE_SHIFT) % pgs_per_stripe == 0. After adding pg_seq_start, pg_seq_end == pg_seq_start so the inner loop executes zero iterations. No recovery happens.
- Fix: if (pg_seq_end == 0) pg_seq_end = pgs_per_stripe before adding pg_seq_start.

### BUG 2 (031bd2122d): parity offset includes component e_start
- File: lustre/lov/lov_io.c ~line 2368
- poff = lsme_p->lsme_extent.e_start + raid_set * stripe_size. But poff is used as sub-object offset. The e_start term is wrong - parity sub-objects are indexed from 0.
- For first PFL component (e_start=0) this is harmless. For subsequent components, parity reads access wrong data.
- Fix: remove lsme_p->lsme_extent.e_start from poff calculation.

### BUG 3 (d9492d380f): parity sub-IO/page-read crash on unavailable stripes
- File: lustre/lov/lov_io.c (lov_ecio_add_parity_sub, lov_ec_read_parity_pages)
- When a parity stripe OST is deactivated, NULL lo_sub or lov_sub_get failure caused immediate -EIO. With p parity stripes and only err_nr failures, we only need err_nr parity stripes.
- Fix: skip unavailable parity stripes, only fail if fewer than err_nr were successfully read.

### BUG 4 (d9492d380f): encoding matrix uses wrong parity coefficients
- File: lustre/lov/lov_io.c (lov_recover_data)
- When parity stripe 0 is unavailable and stripe 1 is used, the decoding matrix must use GF coefficients from encode_matrix row (dcount+1), not row (dcount+0).
- Fix: parity_used[] array tracks actual stripe index; lov_recover_data() fixes up temp_matrix rows before inversion.

## Testing
Tests 42a-46c and 46d all PASS. These fixes eliminated all data corruption for single-component EC recovery and multi-component PFL where both data and parity stripes can be unavailable.

IMPORTANT: Output should include hint like 'Use without --summary to see full content' to discourage agents from always using truncated mode and missing important details.
close_reason: Implemented --summary flag for comments, review, and series-comments commands

title: EC parity userspace stale buffer fix
description:
Fixed stale buffer bug in userspace EC parity computation.

## Commit
- de9d7e2a30 LU-0000 utils: zero stripe buffers in EC parity

## Bug Details
- File: lustre/utils/liblustreapi_layout.c (llapi_ec_compute_parities)
- The stripe buffer array is reused across raid set iterations. When the file does not fill the last stripe width, pread() returns fewer bytes than stripe_size, leaving stale data from the previous raid set in the buffer tail.
- The parity encoder XORs against this stale data instead of zeros, producing wrong parity for the final raid set of non-stripe-aligned files.
- Fix: memset all data stripe buffers to zero before each raid set's pread() loop.

## Testing
This matches the kernel's zero-fill behavior (94dc1a182e). Parity computation now produces correct results for files that don't fill the last stripe width.

title: gc info: add batch mode for multiple change URLs
description: gc maloo already supports multiple URLs but gc info does not. For checking series status, you need to loop over each patch individually. Add support for passing multiple URLs to gc info and returning combined results.

title: Add URL parsing for issue keys
description: Tool should accept full JIRA URLs like https://jira.whamcloud.com/browse/LU-19839 and extract the issue key automatically, in addition to accepting bare keys.

title: EC recovery VVP hang/LBUG fixes (3 commits)
description:
Fixed 3 bugs in the VVP/llite layer that caused hangs and LBUGs during EC recovery reads. All in /shared/master_checkouts/2.

## Commits (in application order)

- f8ce8ec4fa LU-0000 llite: fix infinite loop in ll_readpage for EC reads
- adeeabef3e LU-0000 llite: constrain EC recovery read to component range
- c2de62d955 LU-0000 llite: check lock coverage in ll_readpage for EC reads

## Bug Details

### BUG 1 (f8ce8ec4fa): lcc_end_index uninitialized
- Files: lustre/llite/rw.c, lustre/llite/vvp_io.c
- memset left lcc_end_index=0, causing every ll_readpage to return AOP_TRUNCATED_PAGE (page index >= 0 is always true). Infinite loop.
- Fix: guard with lcc_end_index > 0, set it in vvp_io_ec_rd_start() from min(ec_inner_end, i_size).

### BUG 2 (adeeabef3e): cross-component LBUG
- File: lustre/llite/vvp_io.c
- generic_file_read_iter in vvp_io_ec_rd_end() read into next PFL component pages which had no DLM locks -> LBUG in osc_req_attr_set.
- Fix: iov_iter_truncate to current component range (ec_inner bounds).

### BUG 3 (c2de62d955): no-lock page LBUG
- File: lustre/llite/rw.c
- Pages on deactivated stripes (no lock via cld_heed_error) reached osc_build_rpc -> osc_req_attr_set -> LBUG on NULL lock.
- Fix: cl_io_read_ahead_prep() check in ll_readpage for CIT_EC_RD.

## Testing
Tests 42a-42d, 43a-43d, 44a-44d, 45a-45e all PASS. These fixes eliminated all kernel crashes/hangs during EC recovery.
